problem_statement: Economy and premium_economy seats in column A are being classified as standard instead of window in seat inventory generation, so window-seat preference matching and availability counts are incorrect.
goal: Update seat type classification so that in economy and premium_economy cabins, both A and F columns are treated as window seats when generating seat inventory.
acceptance_criteria: Seat inventory generation classifies 10A (and all A-column seats) as window in economy and premium_economy cabins.
Seat inventory generation continues to classify F-column seats as window in economy and premium_economy cabins.
Seat type classification for other cabin/column combinations remains unchanged.
Window-seat preference matching can select A-column seats in the affected cabins.
Window-seat availability counts include A-column seats in the affected cabins.
constraints: Localized fix should be in backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py, specifically _seat_type_for.
Behavior is limited to economy and premium_economy cabins per issue description.
unknowns: Whether any tests already cover _seat_type_for or seat inventory generation for cabin/column mappings.
Whether business rules differ for other cabin classes beyond economy and premium_economy.
repo_summary: Repo contains a backend-focused seat inventory implementation; the likely logic lives in backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py, with nearby model/schema/migration files in candidate_files.

targeted_symbols.reason: The issue explicitly points to this function as the place where seat type is derived, and the bug is specifically about A-column seats being classified as standard instead of window in economy/premium_economy cabins.

localization_rationale: The bug is deterministic and confined to seat type classification during inventory generation.
The issue description names the affected file and function directly: backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py (_seat_type_for).
The observed failure is a mapping error for cabin/column -> seat_type, not a persistence, schema, or reconciliation problem.
Model/schema/migration files are less likely because the behavior is wrong at generation time, before persistence or API exposure.
